name: pressure_fed_kerosene_lox_system_f790d311
description: Design a full pressure-fed vehicle fluids system for a kerosene/LOX rocket using nitrogen as the pressurant. The system must include: (1) Nitrogen pressurant tank at high pressure, (2) Kerosene fuel tank, (3) LOX oxidizer tank, (4) Pressurant feed lines to both propellant tanks, (5) Fuel feed line from kerosene tank to engine, (6) Oxidizer feed line from LOX tank to engine. FIXED quantities: nitrogen tank initial pressure (typically 20-30 MPa), propellant tank ullage pressures (typically 2-5 MPa), propellant tank temperatures (kerosene ~288K, LOX ~90K). TUNABLE quantities: component sizing, line diameters, exact pressure setpoints, flow rates, tank volumes. The system must maintain positive pressure differential from pressurant to propellant tanks throughout operation, deliver propellants to the engine at required pressures, and maintain cryogenic LOX temperature.

notes: Nitrogen pressurant must maintain higher pressure than both propellant tank ullage pressures throughout the simulation
Pressurant lines should include pressure regulation to maintain target ullage pressures
LOX tank must maintain cryogenic temperature (~90K) throughout operation
Engine injector receives both propellants at appropriate pressures for combustion

checks.description: Simulation completed successfully without crash or invalid configuration
System has non-zero mass flow through connections
No nonphysical or degenerate warnings during simulation
Nitrogen tank starts at high pressure (20-30 MPa range)
Nitrogen tank initial pressure not exceeding 30 MPa
Kerosene tank starts at ambient temperature (~288K)
Kerosene tank initial temperature not exceeding 298K
LOX tank starts at cryogenic temperature (~90K)
LOX tank initial temperature not exceeding 95K
Kerosene tank ullage pressure maintained at minimum 2 MPa
Kerosene tank ullage pressure not exceeding 5 MPa
LOX tank ullage pressure maintained at minimum 2 MPa
LOX tank ullage pressure not exceeding 5 MPa
Pressurant flow from nitrogen to kerosene tank occurs
Pressurant flow from nitrogen to LOX tank occurs
Kerosene feed to engine has non-zero flow
LOX feed to engine has non-zero flow
Nitrogen tank pressure always above kerosene ullage pressure
LOX tank temperature maintained near cryogenic throughout
